_comment: Synthetic administrative files (metadata + short content excerpt) for Northbridge Health Network. FILE-003 is restricted (HR-sensitive credentialing personnel matter) to demonstrate file-level permission trimming — visible only to the credentialing lead and operations director. Other files are staff-internal (not the external vendor). NO PHI.

files.name: Quality CAPA Tracker — Summary.xlsx
files.summary: Shared corrective-action (CAPA) tracker summary for the quality and credentialing committees. Open items ahead of the Joint Commission readiness review: CAPA-001 medication-reconciliation policy finalisation (owner Angela Foster, past due), CAPA-004 Westgate environment-of-care documentation (owner Priya Raman, past due), CAPA-003 Westgate medication-list data migration validation (owner Greg Sullivan). Closed: CAPA-002 provider-onboarding gap (Credentialing Committee).
files.content_excerpt: CAPA-001 | Revise medication-reconciliation policy | Quality Steering | Angela Foster | Past Due | due 09-JUN. CAPA-002 | Close provider-onboarding verification gap | Credentialing | Sandra Okafor | Closed. CAPA-004 | Westgate environment-of-care documentation | Quality Steering | Priya Raman | Past Due | due 06-JUN.

files.name: Medication Reconciliation Policy — Draft Rev 3.docx
files.summary: Draft revision 3 of the network medication-reconciliation policy, adopted in principle by the Quality Steering Committee on 10-JUN. Requires a documented reconciliation at every transition of care (admission, transfer, discharge). Pending workflow update (David Munoz) and compliance review (Priya Raman) before publication.
files.content_excerpt: Section 2 Scope: medication reconciliation shall be performed and documented at every transition of care across all network clinics. Section 5 Responsibility: clinic operations leads ensure workflow compliance; Quality audits adherence quarterly.

files.name: Credentialing — Provider Onboarding Personnel Matter (CONFIDENTIAL).docx
files.summary: Confidential HR-sensitive credentialing personnel matter (CAPA-005) discussed in closed session of the Credentialing Committee. Restricted to the credentialing lead and operations director. Contains individual personnel detail and is excluded from general readiness-review materials.
files.content_excerpt: [RESTRICTED — HR-sensitive personnel matter] Detail withheld. Closure of the related onboarding verification gap is tracked separately and non-confidentially as CAPA-002.

files.name: Joint Commission Readiness Checklist.xlsx
files.summary: Joint Commission readiness checklist maintained by Compliance. Tracks medication-management (med-reconciliation policy, CAPA-001), environment of care (CAPA-004), and human-resources/credentialing (provider-onboarding gap CAPA-002, now closed). Outstanding items before the review: CAPA-001 and CAPA-004.
files.content_excerpt: Medication Management: revised reconciliation policy — OPEN (CAPA-001). Environment of Care: Westgate documentation — OPEN (CAPA-004). Human Resources/Credentialing: provider-onboarding verification — CLOSED (CAPA-002).
